subject: Astronomy & Space
title: New Horizons at Pluto
description: A fast breakdown of New Horizons at Pluto for builders.

hook.text: New Horizons traveled 5 billion km over 9 years to reach Pluto. The flyby lasted just one day.

buildup.text: Before 2015, the best images of Pluto were blurry smudges a few pixels wide.

discovery.text: It found ice mountains taller than the Rockies, nitrogen glaciers, and a vast heart-shaped plain.

twist.text: Pluto has a thin atmosphere that freezes and falls as snow when it drifts farther from the Sun.

climax.text: After Pluto, the probe flew past Arrokoth—a pristine, snowman-shaped rock from the early solar system.

punchline.text: Nine years of travel for one day at Pluto. Worth every km.

quiz.question: What surprising features did New Horizons discover on Pluto?
options: Ice mountains, nitrogen glaciers, and a heart-shaped plain
Active volcanoes and flowing lava rivers
Dense clouds and a thick nitrogen atmosphere
